lesson.title: Spectral Analysis
lesson.body: Spectral analysis represents a time series in the frequency domain. The spectral density f(ω) decomposes variance across frequencies ω ∈ [0, π]. A peak at frequency ω = 2π/s indicates a cycle of period s. The periodogram is the sample estimate of the spectral density. Dominant frequencies correspond to the most important periodic components in the data.

rooms.question: A spectral density with a peak at ω = π/6 corresponds to a cycle of period:
answers: 12 (e.g., months in a year for monthly data)
rooms.explanation: Period s = 2π/ω = 2π/(π/6) = 12. A peak at this frequency indicates annual seasonality in monthly data.

rooms.question: The spectral density is related to the autocovariance function by:
answers: The Fourier transform of the ACVF
rooms.explanation: The spectral density is the Fourier transform of the autocovariance function. Conversely, ACVF is the inverse Fourier transform of f(ω).

rooms.question: For white noise ε_t ~ WN(0, σ²), the spectral density is:
rooms.explanation: White noise has equal variance at all frequencies — a flat spectrum. This is why it is called 'white' (like white light containing all frequencies equally).

rooms.question: Order the steps for spectral analysis of a time series:
items: Ensure series is stationary (difference if needed)
Smooth periodogram to estimate spectral density
rooms.explanation: Stationarity first, then periodogram, smooth it (periodogram is inconsistent), then interpret peaks.

rooms.question: The spectral density of an AR(1) process x_t = φx_{t-1} + ε_t is:
answers: Peaked near ω=0 for positive φ (low-frequency dominance)
Peaked at ω=π for positive φ
rooms.explanation: Positive AR(1): low frequencies dominate (slow mean reversion, long memory). Negative AR(1): high frequencies dominate (fast oscillation).

boss.name: The Frequency Phantom
boss.taunt: In the time domain you see chaos. In frequency, hidden order is revealed.

questions.question: The periodogram I(ωⱼ) at Fourier frequencies is an inconsistent estimator of the spectral density because:
answers: Its variance does not decrease as n grows
questions.explanation: The periodogram's variance stays at f(ω)² regardless of sample size — it is too noisy. Smoothing (kernel, tapering) is needed for consistent estimation.

questions.question: The total area under the spectral density (integrated over [0, π]) equals:
questions.explanation: The variance Var(x_t) = γ(0) equals the total area ∫_{-π}^{π} f(ω)dω = 2∫_0^π f(ω)dω, so each half equals γ(0)/2.

questions.question: Seasonality at period s in a time series appears in the spectrum as:
answers: Peaks at frequencies ω = 2πk/s for k = 1, 2, ..., s/2
questions.explanation: Seasonal frequencies are harmonics: 2π/s, 4π/s, 6π/s, ... Multiple peaks appear, with the fundamental at 2π/s typically strongest.
